why: why no commas between class methods
how: how to add methods to class
how to bind methods in class
compare: arrow function vs regular method in class
output: method is undefined when passed as callback

1_beginner_explanation.summary: Methods inside a class define the actions that instances of that class can perform. You write them immediately after the constructor, without using the `function` keyword and without separating them with commas. Because classes are just Prototypes in disguise, any method you write here is automatically shared across all instances to save memory.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: If the `constructor()` builds the physical robot, the **methods** are the software commands you program into it, like `walk()`, `talk()`, or `attack()`.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: When the engine parses `class A { myMethod() {} }`, it translates it to `A.prototype.myMethod = function() {}`. It also sets these methods to be non-enumerable (they won't show up in `Object.keys()`).
2_internal_mechanism.memory: Standard class methods exist once on the Prototype. If you use Arrow Function properties (`myMethod = () => {}`), they are created uniquely on EVERY single instance, wasting memory.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Defining actions on a User profile.

4_common_confusion.trap: Putting commas `,` between methods inside a class. Classes are not object literals `{}`. Putting a comma will cause a Syntax Error.
4_common_confusion.fix: No commas between methods in a class block.

6_edge_case.quirk: If you pass a standard class method as a callback to an event listener or `setTimeout`, it will lose its `this` context. You must either `.bind(this)` in the constructor, or use an arrow function field.
